title: After-Action Report — Box Score Reformatting + Per-Game Fielding Positions
scope: Two distinct asks emerged: 1. The data model is wrong — box scores show "UT" for half the lineup because the engine never assigns concrete fielding positions to utility players at game time. 2. The presentation is wrong — three styled HTML tables stacked vertically is a database-dump UI, not a box score; the reference target is a 1990s newspaper box score: monospace plaintext, dot leaders, no internal grid lines, sections separated by whitespace.

rationale.item: "Why arn't all 9 fielders (pitcher included) not represented in box scores, then DH separately (jokers) so I know who started and then any pinch hitters or defensjve subs. You need to explore how a baseball box score is designed."
"Nobody should ever be UT in the box score in real life a box score transmits all the info who played where if yoh moved positions jt can show that too"

"Jokers should be J not DH every team has just 3 you cannot sub a joker for another guy only fielders jokers can play in the field to replace someone but you only then have 2 or 1 jokers. This should be a very rare thing"

User pasted a comprehensive spec referencing Henry Chadwick (the inventor of the modern box score in 1858) and the typographic conventions that became the standard format through the 20th century: single <pre> block, dot leaders, fixed column widths (name+pos 22 chars, stats 4 chars right-aligned), no HTML/markdown tables or grid lines, blank-line section separation, W/L/S inline with pitcher name, annotations indented 2 spaces, HR season totals in parens, O27 adaptations (REG/SI1/SI2 phase columns, OS% on pitching, 2C column on batting).
"This box score is rendering as separate HTML tables stacked vertically — three different tables ... Mobile-responsive UI design language, not newspaper box score language. ..."

The position column came straight off players.position — the player's primary position. There's no per-game record. The engine was already doing the position selection implicitly; it just wasn't recording the assignment.

actions.description: Added game_position: str = "" field on Player — assigned at game start by the lineup builder; mid-game defensive moves can extend the string (e.g. "SS-2B"); renderer falls back to position when empty.

New helper _assign_game_positions(starters, sp, dhs) called from _db_team_to_engine: canonical-8 primary positions keep their slot first-come; remaining players greedy best-fit against open slots scored on defense_infield/defense_outfield/defense_catcher; SP gets "P"; jokers get "J" (explicitly not "DH").

game_position TEXT DEFAULT '' column on game_batter_stats with ALTER TABLE migration; _extract_batter_stats and both INSERT paths write it; box-score query exposes box_position with COALESCE fallback to primary position / J for jokers.

New file o27v2/web/box_score.py — pure plaintext newspaper renderer (top rule, title line, line score with REG/SI columns, batting blocks with dot leaders and Totals, annotation lines, pitching tables with inline W/L/S, game notes). Column constants NAME_POS_WIDTH=22, STAT_W=4, PIT_STAT_W=5, RULE_WIDTH=78; helpers _last_name, _pos_short, _name_pos_with_dots, _rj, _rate.

game.html reduced from 472 to 228 lines: removed styled scoreboard panel, styled line-score table, per-phase batting+pitching tables, Game Totals tables, Notable Plays card; replaced with a single <pre> block; Bootstrap nav strip (Prev/Next, JSON export) preserved.
game_detail calls box_score.render_box_score(...) with consolidated row sets and line-score totals; builds a decisions dict ({pitcher_id: "W"|"L"|"S"}) from W/L/SV columns.

Follow-up commit 50d6f77: render.py captures pinch_hit and joker_inserted events; BatterStats gains entry_type (starter | PH | sub | joker) and replaced_player_id fields; persisted via two new game_batter_stats columns with ALTER TABLE migrations and read back in the box-score SELECT.

_ordered_rows_with_indent orders batting rows: starters in lineup order, PH/sub rows indented 2 chars immediately after the starter they replaced (position reads ph), jokers trail un-indented with position j; _name_pos_with_dots accepts an indent parameter.
Season HR totals: one cheap per-side query in game_detail sums HR through this game; annotations renderer formats "Smith 2 (12)" / "Lopez (4)".

risk_posture: Pure rendering — no DB access; takes already-built row sets and emits a single string.
(Legacy games with utility players will still show "UT" until they're re-simmed; new games render correctly.)
None of the rendering or schema changes touch the realism axes the identity contract pins.

validation_claims.claim: tests/test_realism_identity.py still passes 6/6 across both commits.
validation_claims.check_method: pytest tests/test_realism_identity.py at commits b8bffbf and 50d6f77

validation_claims.claim: Sample output shows every position concrete, no UT anywhere, jokers carry the j tag, pitcher's W inline with the name, dot leaders connecting names to stats, right-aligned locked stat columns.
validation_claims.check_method: Render a game page and compare against the sample output in the AAR

non_actions.item: Defensive subs split from PH — everything that flows through the dual-use pinch_hit event is tagged entry_type="PH"
non_actions.reason: A proper "PH" vs "DEF" split would need either a flag on the event or a separate event type. Punt.
the engine doesn't currently emit a pinch-runner event. Would need engine support, then the same render plumbing pattern.

non_actions.item: Joker-into-the-field as defensive sub shown as "J→SS" with joker count dropping 3 → 2
non_actions.reason: The plumbing exists (game_position is a free-form string that can extend), but the trigger event isn't being captured into the stats layer. Punt.
DPs are now tracked at the engine level (per the earlier hits/runs decoupling commits), but they're not surfaced in the box-score annotations yet. Easy follow-up.

non_actions.item: LOB-RISP (left on base with runners in scoring position)

residual_risks: Legacy games with utility players will still show "UT" until they're re-simmed.
open_hooks: game_position free-form string supports mid-game position moves ("SS-2B") and the future J→SS joker-sub display
GIDP annotation is an easy follow-up once surfaced from engine tracking
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Box Score Reformatting + Per-Game Fielding Positions
Follow-up — PH/sub indent + season HR totals (commit `50d6f77`)
unmapped_content: Branch: claude/decouple-hits-runs-rGteJ; Commits: b8bffbf (positions + plaintext box score), 50d6f77 (PH/sub indent + season HR). | Fourth user message: "I'll search for current best practices on baseball box score formatting and Python implementation patterns. Now I have enough context. Here's a directive for an LLM agent on how to build a proper O27 box score in a Python sim ..." (followed by a detailed spec). | Diagnosis SQL: the box-score query at o27v2/web/app.py:1387 selected CASE WHEN p.is_joker = 1 THEN 'J' ELSE p.position END straight off players.position. Screenshot showed SS, RF, LF, UT, CF, UT, C, 1B, P, UT, DH. | _rate formats H/AB to 3 decimals (or above 1.000 if 2C events produced multi-hit ABs — the H/AB column is intentionally unbounded). | Sample box-score output and PH-indent example blocks reproduced in the document. | Season-HR SQL groups game_batter_stats joined to games with (g.game_date < ? OR (g.game_date = ? AND g.id <= ?)). | Diff stats: commit b8bffbf 487 insertions, 262 deletions across 6 files; commit 50d6f77 160 insertions, 13 deletions across 6 files.
parser_notes: Two-commit AAR; user quotes drive the rationale. No explicit delegation decisions beyond the user's directive spec.
